rag_rules: Fix type mismatches by ensuring numeric expressions match expected types (int, real, bool) in AGREE contracts.
Resolve undefined references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; move valid assignments to implementation scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must maintain type consistency across all expressions and declarations.
Only reference identifiers that are explicitly visible in the current component scope.
Place component type contracts in type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
Generated AGREE code should not introduce new package or data type dependencies not already present.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq name : type = expression;` for stateful equations and `assume/guarantee` for logical constraints.
Binary comparisons in AGREE require both operands to be of the same type (int, real, or bool).
Helper declarations and references must be resolvable within the same AGREE annex block.
AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-level features in component type annexes.
Do not assume that AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types not defined in the model.
Do not generate AGREE expressions that reference identifiers not visible in the current scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model fusion and component insertion logic
